title: nauclea orientalis var. macrophylla
intro: Nauclea orientalis var. macrophylla, also known as the "Quinine tree" or "Kayanum," is a variety of the Nauclea orientalis plant species, native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its large leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimalarial, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, malaria, and other infections. The health benefits of Nauclea orientalis var. macrophylla include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off bacterial and fungal infections, and exhibit antioxidant and anticancer properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and the bark and roots are also used in traditional medicine to create remedies for fever and malaria.
